reasoning: The control 8.3.11 focuses on ensuring multi-factor authentication (MFA) for console access, which is a user authentication mechanism typically managed by cloud IAM systems. Kubernetes does not inherently manage user authentication in the same way as cloud providers do. Instead, Kubernetes relies on external identity providers for authentication, such as OIDC, LDAP, or other SSO solutions. While Kubernetes can be configured to use an identity provider that enforces MFA, this is not a Kubernetes-specific control and is instead managed by the external identity provider.
title: Requirement 8.3.11
